word: H5555 - סֶלַע הַמַּחְלְקֹות - Çelaʻ ham-machlᵉqôwth - seh'-lah hammakh-lek-oth' - Sela-hammalekoth

sections.paragraph:
Original: סלע המּחלקות

Transliteration: sela‛ hammachleqôth

Phonetic: seh'-lah ham-makh-lek-oth'

BDB Definition:

Sela-hammahlekoth = "the cliff of escapes" or " the cliff of divisions"
  1. a rock or cliff in the wilderness of Maon, southeast of Hebron, the scene of an escape of David from Saul

Origin: from H5553 and the pl. of H4256 with the article interposed

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech: Proper Name Location
